name: Octagonal tower silhouette
description: Overall silhouette of a tall octagonal tower — a square footprint with the four corners cut to an eight-sided prism, rising well above its width. The chamfered corners read as more refined and less fortress-like than a square keep, and shed wind/arrow angles. Classic of gothic lantern towers, baptisteries, and fantasy mage spires. Interior rooms are rectangular within the octagon; the chamfers are thick wall. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner stacks the floors.
placement_rules: Footprint is an octagon (square with corners chamfered ~1/3 of the side).
Height is 2-4x the width; 3-6 stacked floors.
Interior rooms are rectangular inside the octagon; the corner chamfers are structural wall.
A single vertical stair core serves all floors.
The entrance pierces one of the cardinal (non-chamfered) faces.

gothic.notes: Ashlar lantern tower with tall narrow openings on each face and a steep cap.

fantasy.notes: Mage spire with battered octagonal base and a tapering conical-stepped roof.
